title: paspalum pedicellare
intro: Paspalum pedicellare, also known as Bahamagrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Paspalum pedicellare include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Paspalum pedicellare are typically made by infusing the leaves and stems in water or by decocting them in a solvent, and are often consumed as a tea or used in topical applications.
